subject: GRB 250610C: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 23:11:04 UT on 10 Jun 2025, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 250610C (trigger 771289869.772446 / 250610966).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 26.8, Dec = 59.7 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 01h 47m, 59d 42'), with a statistical uncertainty of 2.5 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 64.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2025/bn250610966/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn250610966.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2025/bn250610966/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn250610966.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2025/bn250610966/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn250610966.gif
